tagline: Earn rewards by putting your phone away.
elevator_pitch: Phone Down is a smart wireless charging pad paired with a gamified app that rewards you for not touching your phone. The longer your phone stays on the pad, the more points you earn, unlocking streaks, competing with friends, and redeeming real rewards from brand sponsors.
problem: Constant phone notifications destroy focus, productivity, and presence. People know they should put their phone away, but willpower alone is not enough. Existing solutions like phone safes feel punitive and offer no positive reinforcement.
solution: Phone Down turns distraction-free time into a game. A sleek wireless charging pad detects when your phone is placed on it and an accompanying app tracks streaks, accelerates point earnings over time, and lets you compete with friends. LED indicators provide visual feedback, and a power hour mode doubles your points when you need deep focus most.
target_market: Knowledge workers, students, and parents aged 18 to 45 who struggle with phone addiction and want a fun, social way to build healthier screen habits.
revenue_model: Hardware sales of the wireless charging pad ($49 to $79), a freemium app with a premium tier ($4.99/month) for advanced analytics and custom power hours, and sponsored rewards from brands who pay to place coupons and offers in the reward store.
key_features: Smart wireless charging pad with LED streak indicators that glow green while earning and red when you pick up
Accelerating point system: the longer your phone stays down, the faster you earn
Power Hour mode for scheduled deep focus sessions with multiplied point rewards
Social leaderboards, friend challenges, and a cosmetic avatar store to spend your points
competitive_advantage: Unlike punitive phone safes or basic screen time trackers, Phone Down uses positive reinforcement, social competition, and tangible rewards to make staying off your phone genuinely fun. The hardware plus software combination creates a physical ritual that pure software cannot replicate.
market_size_estimate: The digital wellness market is projected at $7B+ globally. With over 250 million knowledge workers in North America and Europe, even capturing 1% at $70 average revenue yields a $175M addressable opportunity.
go_to_market: Launch on Kickstarter with a compelling video showing the LED charging pad in action. Seed units to productivity YouTubers and TikTok creators who already discuss focus and screen time. Partner with coworking spaces for bulk orders and sponsored challenges.
risks: Users may game the system by placing their phone on the pad without genuinely disengaging from screens (e.g., using a tablet instead)
Hardware sourcing and manufacturing adds complexity and capital requirements compared to a pure software play
Apple and Android could integrate similar focus-reward features natively, reducing the need for a third-party product
year_one_milestones: Launch Kickstarter campaign and sell 10,000 units in the first 90 days
Ship v1 hardware and app, achieving 5,000 daily active users with 60%+ weekly retention
Sign 5 brand sponsors for the reward store and generate first $50K in sponsorship revenue
startup_cost_range: $150K to $400K (hardware prototyping, mold tooling, app development, and initial inventory run)
